message:
fix(storage): push label filtering into SQL, add timestamp-safe dirty clearing, and harden JSONL reader

Three related storage/sync improvements:

1. Label filtering moved from post-query Rust filtering to SQL EXISTS
   subqueries in both list_issues() and search. This allows LIMIT to work
   correctly with label filters and avoids fetching excess rows.

2. Dirty-flag clearing is now timestamp-aware: get_dirty_issue_metadata()
   returns (id, marked_at) pairs, and clear_dirty_issues() only deletes
   rows whose timestamp matches. This closes a race where a concurrent
   update during export could have its dirty flag incorrectly cleared.
   The old behavior is preserved as clear_dirty_issues_legacy().

3. read_issues_from_jsonl() rewritten to reuse a single String buffer
   via read_line() instead of allocating per line via lines().

4. ExportResult gains exported_marked_at field and Default impl;
   incremental auto-flush updated to use timestamp-safe clearing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
